title: SpaceX shares rise nearly 20% in historic IPO
summary: SpaceX shares surged nearly 20% on their first day of trading following the company's historic IPO, marking a significant milestone for both SpaceX and the space industry.

title: Avanti trims GEO exposure with Hylas-3 sale
summary: Avanti Communications is selling its Hylas-3 satellite to reduce its geostationary orbit exposure. This move marks a shift away from its previous debt-fueled expansion strategy.

title: Astronomers fear orbital data centers will interfere with observations
summary: Astronomers express concerns that SpaceX's upcoming orbital data center satellites, planned for launch as early as next year, may disrupt astronomical observations. The potential interference could hinder scientific research and data collection.

title: All in on AI at Astra
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp suggests he might be the last employee due to the company's strategic focus on AI, rather than financial struggles. The company previously participated in the SPAC boom.

title: H3 successfully returns to flight
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight on June 11, placing six small satellites into orbit after a previous failure in December.

title: In aerospace, AI isn’t replacing workers. It’s filling a shortage
summary: Aerospace executives view AI as a solution to workforce shortages rather than a replacement for human workers, highlighting its role in addressing an overstretched industrial base.

title: AAC Clyde Space wins ESA contract to complete maritime-monitoring constellation
summary: AAC Clyde Space secured a €10.9 million contract from ESA to develop and demonstrate VDES satellites for maritime monitoring. This project aims to enhance the maritime-monitoring constellation.

title: Vandenberg offers new launch site for small and medium rockets
summary: The U.S. Space Force is offering a new launch site at Vandenberg Space Force Base for small and medium rockets. This initiative aims to attract potential users for the dedicated facility.

title: Understanding what’s next for orbital data centers
summary: The article discusses the future of orbital data centers, featuring insights from industry leaders and analysts at a SpaceNews event. It explores the technology and market drivers behind this emerging space industry trend.

title: Senate NDAA backs plan to fold SDA, Space RCO into Space Force
summary: The Senate NDAA proposes folding the Space Development Agency (SDA) and Space Rapid Capabilities Office (Space RCO) into the Space Force, aiming to streamline Pentagon procurement reforms. This move eliminates separate statutory requirements for the two acquisition organizations.

title: Ever have a scary HR meeting on your calendar? That's how the Artemis 3 crew found out their assignments
summary: NASA informed the Artemis 3 crew of their assignments in an unconventional way, akin to a 'scary HR meeting.' The crew was surprised by the announcement of their roles.

title: SpaceX's stock wasn't the company's only launch today — It also put 29 Starlink satellites in orbit
summary: SpaceX launched 29 Starlink satellites aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, coinciding with its NASDAQ stock market debut. The liftoff occurred at 8:27 a.m. ET.

title: Japan's H3 rocket bounces back from failure with successful return to flight launch carrying 6 satellites
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight after a failure last year, carrying six satellites. The mission marks a significant recovery for the country's space program.

title: Could the secret to black hole formation be locked away in this record-breaking ancient quasar?
summary: A record-breaking ancient quasar from 12.9 billion years ago suggests that supermassive black holes could form and age rapidly in the early universe. This discovery may hold clues to understanding black hole formation.

title: Why is the US Space Force researching 'orbital warehouses'?
summary: The US Space Force is researching 'orbital warehouses' to enhance space infrastructure in preparation for two 2027 missions testing spacecraft maneuvering. This initiative aims to address logistical challenges in space operations.

title: 'As terrible and harrowing as things were, people just had to get on with it': 'Star City's' Ruby Ashbourne Serkis and Adam Nagaitis on learning to love the Motherland in Apple TV's soviet space race spin-off (interview)
summary: Apple TV+ is releasing a Soviet space race-themed series titled 'Star City,' featuring actors Ruby Ashbourne Serkis and Adam Nagaitis discussing the show's themes of survival and cultural exploration. The interview highlights the harrowing yet fascinating environment of the Soviet space program.

title: Satellite-boosting spacecraft inside air-launched rocket | Space photo of the day for June 12, 2026
summary: A spacecraft was prepared for encapsulation inside a rocket at a NASA launch facility to assist in saving another satellite. The mission is part of efforts to extend the lifespan of existing satellites.

title: The critical robot arm on the ISS isn't working properly, but NASA has a plan to fix it
summary: A critical robot arm on the ISS is malfunctioning, requiring repairs by astronauts. NASA plans to fix it using a spare already available on the station.

title: My quiet obsession with satellites — and how they're ruining everything
summary: Satellites are increasingly disrupting night sky observations and photography, raising concerns about light pollution and visibility. The article highlights the unintended consequences of satellite proliferation in orbit.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, and LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for China's internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has successfully completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). These veteran astronauts will prepare for the upcoming crewed lunar landing mission.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing for Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: Multiple space launches are scheduled this week, including flights from China, Rocket Lab, and three Starlink missions. The launches will take place from various locations in China, California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent failure of its New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on long-term goals despite the setback.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in advancing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This development marks a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability capabilities.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is assessing damage and potential future risks related to an anomaly. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations following the evaluation.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific space industry news is provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' The test aims to ensure the parachute's functionality under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's Mars chief engineer explains the sterilization process of the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover's parachute, which must be 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone to prevent microbial contamination. The 35-meter parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO will positively impact the broader space industry by attracting more investment. The move could shift reliance from private capital to public markets, benefiting other space companies.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about the kind of economy that will emerge from this investment. The focus is on whether capital will drive operational growth or merely lead to hardware accumulation.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO is a landmark event for the US space industry, with significant implications for Europe's space sector. The move could reshape global competition and investment dynamics in the space economy.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox has launched an AI-focused update to enhance the capabilities of AI agents in geospatial-data analysis. This update aims to improve the effectiveness of AI-driven spatial intelligence.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on the accuracy of SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its potential IPO. The space industry's rapid growth is widely acknowledged despite the valuation debate.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC Sat, entering the orbital data center market. The company aims to leverage space-based infrastructure for data processing and storage.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital has raised $5 million in a pre-seed funding round to develop its ODC constellation. The ODC concept uses a network of smaller satellites to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground communication.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to launch at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite the process.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA confirmed that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lander for a 2027 Earth orbit mission, serving as a test for future Moon landings. The event marked a collaboration between NASA and the two companies ahead of Artemis III.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has selected Arkadia Space to provide a propulsion system for its 2027 satellite mission. This partnership highlights the growing collaboration between European space startups.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX started public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase over its IPO price, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is increasingly recognized as essential infrastructure as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services converge. This integration is critical for enterprises managing fleets, utilities, and remote assets.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for improved international disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to leverage Starlink's satellite internet capabilities for global emergency preparedness.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded contracts worth C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance Earth Observation (EO) satellite capabilities. This move aims to strengthen Canada's satellite-based monitoring and data collection.

title: Viasat Secures PTS-G U.S. Space Force SSC Swarm 1 Contract
summary: Viasat has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command to develop and launch the first satellite in a proliferated fleet of small satellites. The contract is part of the PTS-G Swarm 1 initiative.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, following 24 years as a private company. The move is driven by investor confidence in Elon Musk and expectations of massive AI growth.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has come out of stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer for space. The company aims to build a network to support space transportation and logistics.

title: Space Forge Secures £10 Million of New Investment through European, UK Space Agencies
summary: Space Forge secured £10 million ($13.4 million) in new investment from the European Space Agency and UK Space Agency. The funding is part of ESA's General Support Technology Programme, supported by UKSA's investment.

title: Lithuania and Luxembourg Strengthen Space Defense Ties
summary: Lithuania and Luxembourg are strengthening their collaboration in space defense following a meeting between their respective defense ministers. The partnership aims to enhance security and cooperation in the space sector.
company: Government of Lithuania and Government of Luxembourg

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation (EO) data company, is expanding into Brazil to leverage the country's climate pressures and rapid growth. The move marks a significant step in the company's international expansion.
